hops.context: ResNet-50 attained a higher accuracy of 90% and an F1-score of 0.93 but with a significantly greater computational cost of 80.38 million FLOPs.
PolygoNet (Contours) improved to an F1-score of 0.91 and an accuracy of 83%, both with low computational complexity of approximately 8.5 million FLOPs.
In contrast, ResNet-50 attained a higher accuracy of 90% and an F1-score of 0.93.

question: Which model exhibited higher accuracy on the FashionMNIST dataset despite having a significantly greater computational cost?

options.A: PolygoNet, achieving 90% accuracy with computational costs similar to ResNet-50.
options.B: ResNet-50, achieving an accuracy of 83% and a lower computational cost than PolygoNet.
options.C: PolygoNet, reaching an accuracy of 83% but with only 8.5 million FLOPs.
options.D: ResNet-50, achieving 90% accuracy and requiring 80.38 million FLOPs.

explanation: ResNet-50 was evaluated on FashionMNIST and achieved a higher accuracy of 90%, as stated in the context. It also had a significantly greater computational cost of 80.38 million FLOPs compared to PolygoNet's 8.5 million FLOPs. Despite the higher cost, its performance in terms of accuracy was superior to PolygoNet's 83%.
